mechanism: Sesquiterpene lactones (for example helenalin derivatives) inhibit NF-kB-related inflammatory signaling; topical use is the primary supported route.
safetyNotes: Use externally on intact skin only; oral arnica is potentially toxic and can cause serious cardiovascular and neurologic adverse effects. Avoid in pregnancy and in Asteraceae allergy.

sources.title: Arnica montana for pain and inflammation: systematic review (Rheumatol Int. 2014)
sources.note: Clinical evidence for topical use and limits of efficacy.

interactions: May potentiate: Oral use may increase bleeding risk when combined with anticoagulants or antiplatelet drugs
Caution with: Topical products can irritate skin and may worsen dermatitis when used with other irritants
preparation: Flowers infused in oil or tinctured for topical use only; not taken internally
